trap: Confusing molality (m) with molarity (M). Molality uses kg of solvent, molarity uses L of solution.
Forgetting that Raoult's Law applies ONLY to ideal solutions. Non-ideal solutions show positive or negative deviations.
Using van't Hoff factor (i) incorrectly: i > 1 for dissociation (NaCl), i < 1 for association (acetic acid in benzene).
Mixing up cell EMF sign: E°cell = E°cathode − E°anode, NOT the other way round.
Forgetting to balance electrons when using Nernst equation. The 'n' in the equation is number of electrons transferred.
Confusing order of reaction with molecularity. Order is experimental, molecularity is theoretical and always a whole number.
Using integrated rate law for wrong order. First order: ln[A] vs t is linear, NOT [A] vs t.
Forgetting that chelate complexes are more stable than non-chelate (chelate effect). EDTA forms 6 bonds.
Confusing FCC (4 atoms/unit cell, CN=12) with BCC (2 atoms/unit cell, CN=8).
Mixing up physisorption (weak van der Waals, reversible) with chemisorption (strong chemical bonds, irreversible).
